_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize FL state legislators batch: corey-simon (SD-3), nathan-boyles (HD-3), clay-yarborough (SD-4), patt-maney (HD-4), shane-g-abbott (HD-5). Every TRUE/FALSE cites a source; null where no evidence; never inferred from party. High-value catch: Simon NAY on 6-week abortion ban SB300 (one of two Senate R crossovers).

profile.background: FL Senate District 3 (Tallahassee/Big Bend), in office since 2022, reelected 2024. Former FSU All-American/NFL defensive tackle; ex-CEO Volunteer Florida. Chair, Education Pre-K-12.

corey-simon@FL.notes_set: Evidence-scored 2026-06. Reliable conservative on guns (YEA HB543 permitless carry), school choice (Senate SPONSOR + YEA HB1 universal vouchers), LGBT/gender (YEA SB254 minor gender-care ban, YEA SB1438 anti-drag), elections (YEA SB7050), ESG (YEA HB3), crime (YEA death-penalty bills SB450/SB1804), immigration (YEA HB1451). HIGH-VALUE CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION on life: voted NAY on SB300 6-week abortion ban (one of only two Senate Republicans), publicly favors a 15-week limit with rape/incest exceptions and called 6-week too restrictive -> scored sanctity q0/q1 FALSE on documented record. Notably Florida Right to Life still endorsed him 2024. No evidence found on IVF/euthanasia, Christian-liberty votes, CBDC, 10th-Amendment -> null.

0.note: Voted NAY on SB300 6-week ban; publicly supports 15-week limit WITH rape/incest exceptions and called 6-week too restrictive -- contrary to personhood-from-conception.

1.note: Voted against the strictest available abortion restriction (SB300); no abolition advocacy -- documented contrary.

2.note: Voted YEA on SB254 banning gender-affirming care/procedures for minors.

4.note: Voted YEA on SB1438 'Protection of Children' anti-drag/adult-performance law.

0.note: Senate sponsor and YEA on HB1/SB202 universal school-choice voucher expansion.

1.note: SB254 restricts minors' gender interventions -- aligned with parental authority over minors' gender care.

4.note: Voted YEA on HB3 (2023) anti-ESG 'Combating ESG Activism' law.

3.note: Voted YEA on SB7050 election omnibus restricting third-party voter registration / harvesting-adjacent provisions.

2.note: Voted YEA on SB450 (8-4 jury death threshold) and SB1804 (death penalty for child-sex traffickers) -- proportional punishment of evildoers.

4.note: Capital-punishment votes affirm the magistrate's duty to punish grave evil.

2.note: Voted YEA on HB1451 (2024) barring local governments from accepting non-citizen ID cards; YEA SB4-C state penalties for illegal entry.

0.note: Voted YEA on HB543 (2023) permitless/constitutional carry (primary-source PDF verified).

profile.background: FL House District 3 (Okaloosa/Santa Rosa). Won June 2025 special election; qualified as incumbent for 2026 re-election (June 2026), faces Dem Ingrid Wadsworth. Former 12-year Okaloosa County Commissioner; attorney; owns Adams Sanitation. Homeschools his children.

nathan-boyles@FL.notes_set: Evidence-scored 2026-06. ROSTER/CURRENCY FIX: no longer a county commissioner -- now a sitting FL State Rep (HD-3, won 2025 special), running for re-election 2026 (qualified, not withdrawn). Positions scored from cited campaign 'Issues' self-statements: pro-life ('always protect the unborn'), school choice + homeschools own kids, balanced-budget/low-tax, pro-law-enforcement (police/fire funding + border partnership), deport illegal aliens, defend 2A against restrictions. Mild fiscal nuance: qualifies tax cuts with 'don't cripple local services' (county-commission background). NO EVIDENCE on marriage/LGBTQ, Christian-liberty specifics, elections, CBDC/ESG -> null. Endorsements are business/GOP-establishment (Chamber, NFIB); no single-issue Christian/2A group endorsement found.

0.note: Campaign Issues page: 'stand firm on my pro-life principles... I will always protect the unborn.'
Supports school choice and parental control of education; homeschools his own two children.

2.note: Self-stated: keep taxes low and balance the budget; cites FL's #1 fiscal-health status.

0.note: Pro-law-enforcement: prioritizes protecting police/fire funding and partnering with law enforcement on border security.

1.note: Self-stated: 'deport illegal aliens caught in Florida' and end taxpayer incentives for illegal immigration.
Self-stated: defend 2A; 'no politician passes laws to restrict law abiding citizens' gun rights.'

profile.background: FL Senate District 4 (Nassau + part of Duval/Jacksonville), in office since 2022. Chairs Judiciary Committee. Former FL House member (2016-22) and Jacksonville City Council President. Prolific social-conservative bill sponsor.

clay-yarborough@FL.notes_set: Evidence-scored 2026-06. Strong social conservative (Baptist, official bio). HARD prime/co-sponsorships: SB300 (2023 6-week heartbeat ban, co-introducer, 'strongest pro-life legislation in 50 years'), SB254 (prime sponsor, minor sex-reassignment ban), SB1438 (sponsor, anti-drag 'Protection of Children'), HB529 (2021, co-sponsor, school moment-of-silence/prayer). NRA-endorsed 2022. No cross-party deviation found. Vocal supporter (not confirmed prime sponsor) of 2024 school-chaplains bill. NO EVIDENCE found on economics/elections/10th-Amendment, and his individual roll calls on HB1/SB1718/SB150 were not confirmable -> null.

0.note: Co-introducer of SB300 6-week heartbeat ban; 'honored... to support the strongest pro-life legislation in more than 50 years.'

2.note: Prime sponsor of SB254 banning sex-reassignment prescriptions/procedures for minors.

4.note: Senate sponsor of SB1438 'Protection of Children' anti-drag law.

1.note: SB254 (prime sponsor) restricts minors' gender-care interventions -- parental sovereignty over minors' medical/gender decisions.

3.note: Co-sponsor of HB529 (2021) mandatory daily moment of silence for prayer/meditation in public schools.

1.note: NRA endorsed for state Senate (2022) -- hard evidence of opposition to gun-control restrictions.

profile.background: FL House District 4 (Okaloosa/Fort Walton Beach), in office since 2020; running for re-election 2026 vs Dem John Emerling. Retired U.S. Army Brigadier General (Bronze Star x2, Purple Heart; TBI in Afghanistan 2005) and former Okaloosa County Judge (1989-2018). Chairs Justice Budget Subcommittee.

patt-maney@FL.notes_set: Evidence-scored 2026-06. Near-party-line conservative on the cultural core: cosponsor + YEA HB5 (15-week ban), YEA SB300 (6-week ban), YEA HB1557 Parental Rights in Education, YEA HB543 permitless carry. Ratings: FL Right to Life PAC 100% (2020), ACU-FL 90%, NFIB 100%; NRA-PVF endorsed. NOTED NUANCE (not scored as a FALSE): his judge/general background drives a reform-minded, bipartisan criminal-justice/mental-health portfolio (sponsored Baker/Marchman Act overhaul +$50M, expanded Veterans Treatment Courts, worked with Dem Sen. Lauren Book) -- a deviation in APPROACH, not soft-on-crime, so public_justice cells left null rather than false. NO EVIDENCE on his individual HB1/election/immigration/10th-Amendment votes -> null. Religion not disclosed.

0.note: Cosponsor + YEA on HB5 (15-week ban) and YEA on SB300 (6-week ban); Florida Right to Life PAC 100% (2020).

4.note: Voted YEA on HB1557 Parental Rights in Education -- restricts classroom promotion of sexual-orientation/gender-identity content.

1.note: HB1557 (YEA) includes parental-notification requirements on student health/wellbeing services.

2.note: HB1557 (YEA) restricts SOGI classroom instruction in K-3 / age-inappropriate grades.

0.note: Voted YEA on HB543 (2023) permitless/constitutional carry.

1.note: NRA-PVF endorsed (2022) -- hard evidence of opposition to gun-control restrictions.

profile.religion: Destiny Worship Center, Freeport FL (member); member of The Gideons; FCA board member
profile.background: FL House District 5 (Panhandle: Calhoun, Holmes, Jackson, Walton, Washington), in office since 2022, reelected 2024, running 2026. Pharmacist; co-owns The Prescription Place. UF pharmacy degree.

shane-g-abbott@FL.notes_set: Evidence-scored 2026-06. Consistent conservative with a strong faith profile (Destiny Worship Center, Gideons, FCA). HARD votes: YEA SB300 6-week ban (+ floor speech on protecting unborn human life), YEA SB1674 biological-sex bathroom bill, YEA HB1 universal vouchers, YEA HB3 2024 minor age-verification, YEA HB931 school chaplains, YEA HB3 2023 anti-ESG, YEA SB7050 elections, YEA SB1718 E-Verify/immigration + HB1451 (deny IDs to illegal migrants), CO-SPONSOR + YEA HB543 permitless carry, YEA age-18 firearm-purchase bills, NRA member/endorsed. No cross-party deviation; one intra-conservative split (NO on HCR693 Article V/term-limits convention) not mapped. NO EVIDENCE on public-justice/CBDC/faith-based-adoption -> null.

0.note: Voted YEA on SB300 6-week ban and gave floor speech contrasting protections for unborn humans vs. wildlife eggs.

2.note: Voted YEA on SB1674 (2023) requiring restroom use based on biological sex.

0.note: Voted YEA on HB1 (2023) universal school-choice vouchers.

3.note: Voted YEA on HB3 (2024) minor online age-verification / protection from harmful content.
Voted YEA on HB931 (2024) authorizing volunteer school chaplains -- religious presence in public schools.

4.note: Voted YEA on HB3 (2023) anti-ESG 'Combating ESG Activism' law.

3.note: Voted YEA on SB7050 election omnibus restricting third-party voter registration; YEA HB1205 petition restrictions.

2.note: Voted YEA on SB1718 (anti-sanctuary/immigration enforcement) and HB1451 (deny IDs issued to illegal migrants).

3.note: Voted YEA on SB1718 mandating E-Verify for employers.

0.note: CO-SPONSOR and YEA on HB543 (2023) permitless/constitutional carry.

1.note: Voted to lower firearm-purchase age back to 18 (rolling back post-Parkland restriction); NRA member/endorsed.
